title: violation model: obligation + debug assert + compile-out
manager: The unified model for what a contract violation is and does: at heart an obligation, checked as a debug assertion during development, and compiled out entirely under a sealed or proven build once the checker has discharged it. This is what lets contracts be everywhere in development without paying for them in production — you write guarantees liberally, and proof rather than faith decides which ones still cost runtime.
cs: violation = obligation; runtime debug-assert tier; compile-out under sealed/proven once the checker discharges it; the shared semantics for require/ensure/invariant (W6.62-.64).
